description: Shared SDK conformance scenario (issue #22). One ordered control-plane scenario both the Python SDK (sdk/python/mitos) and the TypeScript SDK (sdk/typescript) run against the SAME standalone sandbox-server in mock mode. Each runner asserts that its normalized result equals the expectation below, so the two languages prove byte-equal logical behavior for the shared control plane: create template, list templates, fork, list sandboxes, terminate. Exec / files / run_code are NOT in scope here: the mock engine has no guest VM (no vsock guest agent), so the conformance SURFACE is the control plane only; the in-VM data path is proven on the KVM CI job (kvm-test.yaml).

normalization.doc: Normalization makes the two languages assert byte-equal logical results regardless of language casing or transport details. RULES: (1) compare only the stable keys named below; (2) ignore all timing fields (creation_time_ms / fork_time_ms / created_at / createdAt) since they vary per run; (3) the SDK-native camelCase keys (templateId, creationTimeMs) and the wire snake_case keys (template_id, creation_time_ms) are normalized to the snake_case keys used in this file; (4) endpoint is normalized to the boolean endpoint_present (the value is the server's own URL and is environment dependent), so both SDKs assert only that an endpoint string is present and non-empty.
